arm_description: Sonnet 4.6 with adaptive extended thinking at max effort. Comparison arm to Opus 4.7.

pre_registration.note: MUST match pre_registration.lock.methodology_hash. compare_arms.py refuses to run if any arm's hash differs.

materials.note: MUST match pre_registration.lock.materials_lock_hash. Identical to opus-4-7 arm — same corpus, same ground truth, same noise pool.

analyst.note: max_output_tokens raised from initial 32768 after baseline smoke test showed Sonnet at effort=max uses ~17K thinking tokens, leaving insufficient budget for the 8-answer JSON body. 65536 confirmed safe across all 91 runs.

pricing_note: Snapshotted to allow honest cost reconstruction even if API pricing changes after this arm closes.

execution_results.grade_unparseable_note: Pre-existing parser bug — concentrated on Q8 (MSFT-S-03) framework synthesis. Same systemic issue exists on opus-4-7 arm at similar rate (16/329 = 4.9% on Opus, 21/273 = 7.7% on Sonnet). Filtered out of cross-arm analysis since it equally affects both arms.

git_anchor.note: Tag points to the commit that locks this arm. Full reproducibility from `git checkout arm/sonnet-4-6/data-v1.0`.

execution_notes: Pilot (3 cells × 7 reps = 21 runs) ran 2026-04-25 12:31-14:33, $137. One baseline run failed initially with httpx.RemoteProtocolError; api.py retry classifier was extended to cover transient streaming errors and the run was retried successfully.

Full execution (remaining 70 runs) ran 2026-04-25 15:01-19:36, $169 collect + $4 extract + $213 grade. Zero unrecoverable failures — the broadened retry classifier handled multiple transient API hiccups, including a retry storm during grade (~20 retries in 2 minutes, all recovered).
Sonnet 4.6 supported effort levels: low, medium, high, max. Opus 4.7 has 5 levels (adds xhigh). Both arms used max — apples-to-apples preserved.
Sonnet exposes thinking_text + usage.thinking_tokens via SDK; Opus 4.7 redacts text and exposes signature_chars. Both fields captured in api.py.
Sonnet at effort=max consistently allocates ~12-18K thinking tokens per call, vs Opus's ~1-3K on the same prompt. Behavioral difference, not methodology issue.
